reasoning: The target threshold (10x the historical average) requires approximately 86 protests in a 30-day window, or roughly 3 per day. Our search results indicate that while there was a significant surge of protests in late October and November 2025 following the contested presidential elections, this movement was heavily suppressed by state security forces. Following typical post-election trajectories in similar contexts, the active street protests have dissipated. Searches for ongoing protests in January and February 2026 yield no evidence of a sustained mass movement of the scale required to clear 86 events per month. "Ghost town" strikes may be ongoing, but generally do not qualify as ACLED "Protests" unless three or more individuals engage in a physical, in-person demonstration. Given the 30-day window for the first resolution date lies largely in the past/present with no signs of mass demonstrations, the first date resolves NO with near certainty. The second date (March 17) has a slightly longer runway but lacks any precipitating triggers in current events, maintaining a very low probability.
